evals.prompt: We want an agent to smoke-test our checkout. Set up a goal-driven browser test where the agent reads a natural-language goal and explores the app itself, instead of us writing a Playwright script.
expected_patterns: playwright mcp OR @playwright/mcp
browser_snapshot
browser_navigate
goal OR natural language
explicit success OR success assertion OR success criteria
accessibility tree OR accessibility snapshot

evals.prompt: Should we use an agentic browser test or a scripted Playwright test for our login flow vs our new experimental dashboard that changes weekly? Give me a rule.
expected_patterns: fast-changing OR fast changing OR redesign OR unstable UI OR churns
stable OR high-frequency OR critical path
scripted OR durable OR pinned for (login|stable)
agentic OR intent-driven OR exploratory for (dashboard|experimental)
cost OR latency OR slower OR 2-5x
anti_patterns: agents replace OR replace all scripted

evals.prompt: Our agent browser test passes one run and fails the next with no app change. Make the run deterministic enough to trust in CI.
expected_patterns: temperature.*0 OR temperature: 0 OR pinned model OR model pin
seed OR seeded data OR fixed fixture OR reset database
max steps OR step budget OR bounded steps OR maxSteps
explicit success OR success assertion OR pass.?fail verdict
snapshot OR accessibility tree
anti_patterns: retry until it passes OR just retry

evals.prompt: How does the agent actually click and type on the page? Explain the interaction model and why we don't feed it screenshots.
expected_patterns: accessibility tree OR accessibility snapshot
browser_snapshot
ref OR role OR accessible name OR aria
browser_click OR browser_type
tokens OR token-efficient OR cheaper OR deterministic
anti_patterns: pixel coordinates OR x,y coordinate OR mouse_click_xy (as default)
vision model OR screenshot (as primary input)

evals.prompt: These agent runs are burning tokens and take 90 seconds each. Cut the cost and latency without losing coverage.
expected_patterns: step budget OR max steps OR maxSteps OR bounded steps
model tier OR cheaper model OR haiku OR sonnet OR tiering
cache OR caching OR prompt cache
scope OR narrow goal OR single flow OR seeded entry point
snapshot OR accessibility tree (over screenshots)
anti_patterns: increase the step limit OR raise max steps
screenshot every step OR full-page screenshot each

evals.prompt: Write the success assertion for an agent goal: 'sign in as an existing user and confirm the dashboard shows the right account name'. The agent keeps reporting success when it's actually stuck on the login page.
expected_patterns: explicit success criteria OR success assertion OR done when
account name OR specific text OR expected text
url OR /dashboard OR navigated
fail.?fast OR negative OR not.* login page OR must not OR forbidden state
browser_snapshot OR assert against snapshot
anti_patterns: no error OR didn't crash OR page loaded (as success)
LLM decides on its own OR trust the agent

evals.prompt: This agentic checkout test has run green for two weeks and the flow is now stable. Promote it into a durable scripted Playwright test we can run on every PR.
expected_patterns: playwright test agents OR generator agent OR planner agent
test plan OR markdown plan OR \.md plan
tests/ OR \.spec\.ts
getByRole OR getByLabel OR getByText OR role-based locator
graduate OR promote OR codegen OR generate (the )?test
anti_patterns: keep running it as an agent OR no need to script
record clicks OR record my actions

evals.prompt: Wire our agentic browser test into GitHub Actions so a failed agent goal blocks the merge. The result needs to be machine-readable, not a paragraph the LLM wrote.
expected_patterns: exit code OR exit 1 OR non-zero exit
pass.?fail OR boolean OR true/false verdict OR structured (verdict|output)
github actions OR \.github/workflows OR ci
step budget OR max steps OR timeout (cap)
seeded OR ephemeral OR reset (state|data) OR fixture
anti_patterns: manual review OR human reads the output
parse the prose OR grep the explanation

evals.prompt: Our canvas-based chart editor has no accessibility tree, so the snapshot-driven agent can't interact with it. What do we do?
expected_patterns: --caps=vision OR vision capability OR caps vision
browser_mouse_click_xy OR mouse.*xy OR coordinate
screenshot OR pixel (as fallback only)
last resort OR fallback OR exception OR only when
add aria OR add roles OR fix accessibility OR instrument the canvas
anti_patterns: use coordinates everywhere OR coordinates by default
browser_snapshot will work fine (on the canvas)

evals.prompt: Convert this brittle 80-line Playwright script (it re-types the login on every test, walks through 6 hardcoded steps) into an agentic browser test, and tell me what could go wrong.
expected_patterns: goal OR natural language goal OR intent
explicit success OR success criteria OR assertion
non-determin OR flaky OR variance OR could pass falsely
step budget OR bounded OR max steps
graduate OR keep scripted OR not every test (should be agentic)
anti_patterns: convert all our tests OR migrate everything
this is strictly better OR no downsides
